---
name: Specification issue
about: Disagreement on a specification mapping or epistemic labeling
title: "[SPEC] "
labels: specification
assignees: ''
---

## Location

Which assertion(s) or mapping(s) are concerned.

- File : `<spec corpus path>`
- ID(s) : EC-... / Z-...
- Epistemic level currently asserted : E?

## Primary source citation

Cite the primary text(s) supporting your view. Include :

- Edition (with full bibliographic reference).
- Section / folio reference.
- Original-language quote.
- Translation.

## Disagreement

Explain the disagreement. Typical categories :

- Mistranslation or over-translation.
- Incorrect epistemic level (e.g. E1 claimed but actually E3 derivation).
- Attribution error.
- Missing `divergence_note` when sources diverge.
- Synthesis that crosses traditions without warrant.

## Proposed resolution

What the corrected mapping / label should look like.

## Editorial context

Any additional editorial context (manuscript variants, commentator disputes) relevant to the resolution.
